scenario: Server hung while trying to acquire spinlock by third party module seh_vhcd
symptoms: Server hung trying to acquire spinlock by third party module [seh_vhcd]
watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#1 stuck for 22s! [lsusb:2011933]

workflow.thought: Check task state distribution to understand system load
workflow.observation: 78 RU tasks and 4 UN tasks contributing to load

workflow.thought: Examine run queue lengths to identify CPU congestion
workflow.observation: CPU 1 has 66 tasks in run queue causing load elevation

workflow.thought: Inspect run queue contents for CPU 1 to see waiting tasks
workflow.observation: Detailed list of 66 tasks waiting in CPU 1 run queue

workflow.thought: Get backtrace of lsusb task to identify hang location
workflow.observation: Task stuck at smp_call_function_single+210 exception

workflow.thought: Disassemble the faulting instruction for detailed analysis
workflow.observation: Instruction at smp_call_function_single+210 shows mov (%rsp),%edx

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for lockup messages to confirm soft lockups
workflow.observation: 212 lockup messages in system log

workflow.thought: Examine recent lockup messages to identify affected processes
workflow.observation: Multiple soft lockups for lsusb process on CPU 1

workflow.thought: Check for RCU stalls indicating synchronization issues
workflow.observation: 179 rcu_sched stall messages in log

workflow.thought: Examine recent RCU stall details for starvation information
workflow.observation: rcu_sched kthread starved for millions of jiffies

workflow.thought: Lookup symbol address to identify kernel function
workflow.observation: Address corresponds to do_flush_tlb_all function

workflow.thought: Check runqueue latency to identify stalled CPUs
workflow.observation: CPU 0 shows 49256.36 secs latency indicating severe stall

workflow.thought: Set context to CPU 0 to examine the stalled task
workflow.observation: Task utnservice (PID 1450) is running on CPU 0

workflow.thought: Get backtrace of utnservice task to find hang cause
workflow.observation: Task stuck in vhci_get_from_hcd_tx_queue from seh_vhcd module

workflow.thought: Examine spinlock function address for reference
workflow.observation: _raw_spin_lock_irqsave function address is 0xffffffffa83f1170

workflow.thought: Disassemble third party function to see spinlock call
workflow.observation: Function calls _raw_spin_lock_irqsave at offset 158

workflow.thought: Disassemble kprobe handler for context
workflow.observation: kprobe_ftrace_handler function prologue

workflow.thought: Search backtrace for spinlock calls with context
workflow.observation: Stack frame shows _raw_spin_lock_irqsave call context

workflow.thought: Examine raw_spinlock_t structure at suspected address
workflow.observation: Spinlock shows counter=0, locked=0, not currently held

workflow.thought: Lookup symbol for third party function to confirm module
workflow.observation: Function is within seh_vhcd module code

workflow.thought: Check module taints to identify unsigned modules
workflow.observation: seh_vhcd module has OE taint

workflow.thought: Get module details including signature status
workflow.observation: seh_vhcd module base address and size

workflow.thought: Examine module structure for signature verification
workflow.observation: Module sig_ok = false, indicating unsigned module

root_cause_analysis: The task was trying to acquire the spinlock inside the function vhci_get_from_hcd_tx_queue().
However, the function vhci_get_from_hcd_tx_queue() is part of a third party [seh_vhcd] kernel module.
